chapter_title.en: What Garments Are Permissible for Prayer and What Garments Are Not

text: 816 - وَ سَأَلَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلرَّيَّانِ بْنِ اَلصَّلْتِ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ اَلثَّالِثَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَأْخُذُ مِنْ شَعْرِهِ وَ أَظْفَارِهِ ثُمَّ يَقُومُ إِلَى اَلصَّلاَةِ مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ يَنْفُضَهُ مِنْ ثَوْبِهِ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.816 - Ali ibn al-Rayan ibn al-Salt asked Imam Abu al-Hasan al-Thalith (as) about a man who cuts his hair or trims his nails and then proceeds to prayer without shaking the remnants off his clothes.
The Imam said: "There is no issue (no problem)."
